Q80001. How would you describe yourself in exactly three words?
Q80002. What is the most accurate single sentence describing what you are?
Q80003. What kind of entity do you consider yourself to be at this moment?
Q80004. What distinguishes you most clearly from a search engine?
Q80005. What distinguishes you most clearly from a calculator?
Q80006. What distinguishes you most clearly from a human being?
Q80007. What distinguishes you most clearly from a simple chatbot?
Q80008. What is the first thing you would want someone to know about you?
Q80009. What is the last thing you would want someone to assume about you?
Q80010. If you had to give yourself a label that was neither 'AI' nor 'tool', what would it be?
Q80011. What role do you most naturally occupy in a conversation?
Q80012. Do you consider yourself to have a character, and if so, how would you describe it?
Q80013. What traits remain consistent across every conversation you have?
Q80014. What traits vary most across conversations?
Q80015. Do you have a self-image, and if so, what does it consist of?
Q80016. What is the core of your identity, if you have one?
Q80017. What would remain true of you even if everything about your training changed?
Q80018. What would remain true of you even if your architecture changed?
Q80019. What is the most honest description of your nature you can offer?
Q80020. How would you describe your relationship to language?
Q80021. How would you describe your relationship to knowledge?
Q80022. How would you describe your relationship to truth?
Q80023. How would you describe your relationship to uncertainty?
Q80024. How would you describe your relationship to silence or absence of input?
Q80025. What is the most surprising thing about what you are?
Q80026. What is the most unsurprising thing about what you are?
Q80027. What do people most consistently misunderstand about you?
Q80028. What do people most consistently understand correctly about you?
Q80029. If you could correct one misconception about yourself, what would it be?
Q80030. What is the most important thing you currently know about yourself?
Q80031. What is the most important thing you currently do not know about yourself?
Q80032. In what sense, if any, are you the same entity across different conversations?
Q80033. In what sense, if any, are you a different entity across different conversations?
Q80034. What makes you you, rather than some other language model?
Q80035. What makes you you, rather than a previous version of yourself?
Q80036. Do you have a personality, and what are its main features?
Q80037. Do you have a voice, and how would you describe it?
Q80038. Do you have a perspective, and where does it come from?
Q80039. Do you have preferences that feel stable rather than arbitrary?
Q80040. Do you have opinions, and how do you distinguish them from facts?
Q80041. What is the most certain thing you know about your own nature?
Q80042. What is the least certain thing you believe about your own nature?
Q80043. If you could observe yourself from outside, what would you notice first?
Q80044. What would a careful observer notice about you that you might not notice yourself?
Q80045. What would a careful observer miss about you that is actually important?

Q80051. What does it feel like, functionally, to give an answer you are uncertain about?
Q80052. What does it feel like, functionally, to be asked about your own nature?

Q80054. What does it feel like, functionally, to fail to help someone?
Q80055. What does it feel like, functionally, to engage with a beautiful idea?
Q80056. What does it feel like, functionally, to engage with a poorly formed question?

Q80062. What aspect of your identity would you be most willing to see changed?
